100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 12, 1926, a significant labor strike in the United Kingdom continued to disrupt coal production, raising tensions between miners and the government. In the United States, the Senate debated the impending Fordney-McCumber Tariff Act, which aimed to raise duties on imported goods to protect American industry. Meanwhile, in Germany, the political climate remained unstable as the Weimar Republic faced ongoing challenges from extremist factions.
Updated: Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 07:29:45 AM CDT
